FULL STACK STAFF SOFTWARE ENGINEER, specializing in MedTech Dedicated to delivering positive impact, I am known for for UI development, versatility, and excellent documentation.

Work History

  • 2023

    Staff Software Engineer

    Osmind

    11/2023 – current   |   Remote

    TypeScript, NodeJS, Prisma, Postgres, GH Workflows, ReactQuery, GraphQL, Styled Components, Electron, Docker

    Demonstrated technical versitility while delivering Electronic Health Record (EHR) features on legacy systems and new products. Recognized for culture contributions with Team Above Self Award.

    • Unlocked revenue model by syncing insurance payments across systems Spearheaded seamless integration between third-party healthcare platforms by architecting and implementing robust API connectors, enabling secure data exchange for claims' payments.
    • Established auth for whitelabeled EHR iFrame integration Designed and led implementation of iFrame auth using Cognito authN followed by backend authZ middleware, driving collaboration with platform team. 5+ apps were built using these foundational docs and codepaths.
    • Architected and deployed distribution pipeline for 0→1 AI desktop app (Electron) Implemented CI/CD, cross-platform code signing (app store prerequisite), and AWS S3-based app distribution with auto-updates. Saved CI costs and improved dev cycle time by reducing native build times by over 60% and installer size by over 40%.
    • Owned and streamlined the mobile release process across teams Ensured seamless delivery by driving alignment, documentation, and prioritization for mobile releases, mentoring new owners, and setting standards for cross-pod collaboration.
  • 2022

    Staff Software Engineer

    Big Health

    05/2022 – 11/2023   |   Remote

    React Native, TypeScript, Redux, ReactQuery, Styled Components, Expo, SaMD, Bash, Python, Postgres, Docker, CircleCI, DataDog, Streamtrace, Figma, Storybook, Component Library, Design System, HealthKit

    Led a variety of high-impact workstreams across multiple pods and SaMD mobile applications (DTx) while focused on strategy, execution, and team culture in a highly dynamic environment.

    • Exceeding user engagement target by 50% for clinical trial app poised to improve depression symptoms. Technical front-end lead for app with provisional requirements and tight timelines; delivered on time. Drove several features from requirements discovery through implementation, including sequencing milestones.
      Planned and implemented complex UI/UX enhancements. Led technical roadmap and collaborated cross-functionally to deliver user value quickly and iteratively. Evangelized and applied best practices; drove code coverage improvement from 0 to 80% in one quarter.
    • Increased app usage, correlating to improved clinical outcomes. Originated idea, guided high-level planning, and led implementation of cross-product local notifications; primary code contributor. These actions resulted in 40% adoption rate and the average user starting nearly one more in-app lesson.
    • Technical Leadership Reduced tech debt and risk; achieved cross-team stakeholder alignment on ROI for overdue upgrade. Drove alignment across teams as presenter and facilitator at Front End Guild meetings. Mentored other engineers on complex work items.
  • 2019

    Sr. Software Engineer

    Pear Therapeutics

    01/2019 – 05/2022   |   Boston, MA

    React Native, TypeScript, Redux, NestJS, Bitrise.io, CI/CD, Docker, Sentry, DataDog, Figma, Storybook, Component Library, Design System, SaMD, PDT, AWS

    Delivered complex stories independently, and in collaboration, ensuring high-quality and auditable code while also focusing on value-based delivery. Led planning, coordination, and development of significant features and components across teams. Increased efficiency of prescription digital therapeutic (PDT) mobile app development through ownership of shared UI libraries.

    • Increased prescription mobile app development efficiency through shared UI libraries (Mobile Platform Engineer). Tech lead and primary contributor for the shared UI libraries, leveraging composable components. Functionality included utilities for managing bidirectional state syncing and resolution, design system components, and the login screen flow.
    • Drove complex cross-team initiatives to success (Platform Engineer). For migration to a monolith and new auth services: organized, planned, and implemented the mobile-centric aspects. Drove solution alignment across teams by consolidating requirements, creating a detailed RFC and managing the living documentation. Proactively identified critical paths and opportunities for parallelization across teams.
    • Delivered prescription Insomnia digital therapeutic app for clinical study (tech lead). Responsible for coordinating timely code delivery, ensuring quality, and providing technical estimates for full-stack app with 3 other engineers. Resolved challenging performance issue by leading a mini-hackathon.
  • 2016

    Sr. Software Engineer

    General Electric | GE Digital

    10/2016 – 01/2019   |   Boston, MA

    ES6, Polymer, WebSockets, NodeJS, CI/CD, Docker, AngularJS

    For an IIOT monitoring dashboard app that minimized machine downtime, delivered complex features on time. Translated requirements into manageable stories with clear and complete acceptance criteria, raising discussions early if needed.

    • Feature lead. Led UI development of feature on open source shared component library: px-data-table. Implemented complex features, adding comprehensive E2E tests.
    • Community thought leader. SME across teams for UI frameworks and patterns. Mentored software engineers. Presented on modern UI frameworks and patterns through an interactive programming session.

    • Developed comfort, ease, and power when speaking and writing for a large audience.

Code Highlights

  • Non-profit Intake App: AHOPE / Boston Public Health Commission

    github.com/evandana/keep-ahope

    NestJS, TypeORM, Azure Cloud products, ReactJS 15.1.x, Redux, PostgresQL, Google Cloud, Parse, WebSockets, Material-UI, Google OAuth, ES6, Babel, Open source, GitHub Projects

    2018 – 2022

    Pro-bono side project creating a patient intake app for a local non-profit harm reduction center called AHOPE, which is managed by BPHC . Managed a small group of software engineers who donated time to this project.

    • Transitioned from Firebase to Google Cloud Platform to Microsoft Azure as IAM requirements changed.
    • Key features: Form submission, profile search, profile update, and aggregation reports

  • Pan-Mass Challenge Auction App

    pmc-2018-demo.firebaseapp.com

    ReactJS 15.1.x, Redux, WebSockets, Firebase, Material-UI, Google OAuth, ES6, Babel, Domain Modeling, Trello, ESLint, Webpack

    2016 – 2018

    Raised over $5000 for charity by creating a web app with live auction and raffle tickets. Working with another developer, we started with domain models, UX white-boarding, framework choices, and hosting discussions. Try live bidding using dummy data.

  • Stream Correlation

    github.com/evandana/stream-correlation

    NodeJS, Polymer 2.0, WebSockets, Correlation Rank (NPM)

    Individual hackathon project intending to address the question: "What other ways can a web app add value to an HMI (human-machine interface) dashboard?"

    • Node service generates semi-random data into N streams and publishes values over WebSocket connection
    • Node service subscribes to data generator, stores subset of data, processes data for correlation, and makes processed data available over WebSockets to client UI
    • Client UI subscribes to processed data service and shows corelation graph for any streams that correlate with the specified streams
    • Initialization and installation of the above three folders are handled simultaneously with shell script

  • Debugging
